summary: Hermes Professional has typed trust contracts and Local V1 workflows for source-grounded legal Q&A, external-source capture, shareholder penetration, Word Add-in handoff, and scoped preference learning. The external-source workflow has a deployable allowlisted HTTPS connector with per-matter authorization, DNS public-address screening, pinned requests, redirect/content/size limits, retained hashes, and a mandatory reviewable workpaper handoff. Shareholder graphs now retain multiple beneficial-owner branches over a direct shareholder, each source-backed and subject to the same review/approval audit. The Word surface has a native Office.js task-pane manifest that reads the current selection and creates the same matter-linked review/audit handoff without Word write permissions. Legal Q&A, shareholder graphs, and Word handoffs require linked review resolution and persisted audit evidence before a local accepted transition. An opt-in, revocable preference candidate now maps only after linked-review acceptance to a newly approved, matter-scoped playbook; it never silently changes other matters or auto-applies.
contractsChanged: allowlisted HTTPS external-source connector with pinned public-address fetch policy
local shareholder graph review-resolution and approval transition
legal_qa_answer persisted work-product kind and local approval transition
word_addin_handoff persisted work-product kind and local approval transition
Office.js Word task-pane manifest and read-only selection capture
matter-scoped output_preference candidate with opt-in, revocation, and no auto-apply
testsRun: cd frontend && rm -rf /tmp/aletheia-adapter-tests && npx tsc -p tests/agentops/tsconfig.adapter.json && node --test /tmp/aletheia-adapter-tests/tests/agentops/anduParity.test.js (8 tests passed)

cd frontend && rm -rf /tmp/aletheia-adapter-tests && npx tsc -p tests/agentops/tsconfig.adapter.json && node --test /tmp/aletheia-adapter-tests/tests/agentops/anduParity.test.js /tmp/aletheia-adapter-tests/tests/agentops/handoff.test.js /tmp/aletheia-adapter-tests/tests/agentops/exportPackage.test.js /tmp/aletheia-adapter-tests/tests/agentops/adapters.test.js /tmp/aletheia-adapter-tests/tests/agentops/skillsEval.test.js (40 tests passed)
cd backend && npm run check:aletheia:operator (ok true, dirty-worktree warning only)
cd backend && npm run check:aletheia:audit-integrity (ok true; default data directory has no matter data)
cd backend && npm run check:aletheia:external-source-connector (allowlist, HTTPS-only, public-address, pinned-fetch, opt-in, and content-type checks passed)
cd backend && npm run check:aletheia:word-addin-manifest (Word manifest and no-write Office.js task-pane checks passed)

cd frontend && npx playwright test tests/aletheia-external-source-workpaper.spec.ts --config=playwright.config.ts --project=desktop-chromium (legal Q&A, external-source, graph, Word handoff approval, and preference candidate passed)

cd frontend && npx playwright test tests/aletheia-external-source-workpaper.spec.ts --config=playwright.config.ts --project=mobile-chromium (legal Q&A, external-source, graph, Word handoff approval, and preference candidate passed)
risks: Legal Q&A, shareholder penetration, and Word handoff are Local V1 workflows; the Supabase adapter deliberately fails closed for their acceptance transitions.
The shareholder graph supports retained multi-beneficial-owner branches but does not yet ingest registry data or resolve contradictory source claims automatically.
The external connector remains deliberately allowlist-only. Deployment must configure licensed/approved domains and run live-source acceptance tests before any automated verification claim.
The Word task pane is manifest-validated but needs an HTTPS deployment and live Word desktop/web host smoke test before organizational distribution.
Preference learning must remain opt-in, scoped, revocable, inspectable, and human-approved; it has no cross-matter or auto-apply path.
needs: Configure approved external-source domains per deployment and run live-source acceptance against each licensed source before enabling automatic capture.
Deploy the Word task pane to a trusted HTTPS origin, replace localhost manifest URLs, and execute a real Office-host smoke test before catalog distribution.
Add registry ingestion, structured ownership percentages, and source-conflict states before representing any shareholder result as registry-verified.
Design, consent, and evaluate any future automatic application separately; do not infer it from a preference candidate or approved playbook.
